title: Macros & Calories Breakdown of My Animal-Based Diet

chunks.title: Introduction to Animal-Based Diet
chunks.summary: The speaker outlines their current animal-based diet, which includes meat, organs, raw dairy, fruit, honey, and sometimes squash. They plan to break down the nutritional aspects using a tracking tool called chronometer and later compare their diet to a well-constructed vegan diet.
chunks.transcript: what do I eat in a day in November of 2024 if you guys saw my previous video which I'll put a link to I've done a video all about how I'm eating right now it's still an animal- based diet focused on animal products meat organs and some raw dairy along with fruit and honey and part of that is sometimes some squash which is actually a fruit but in this video I want to show you a breakdown of nutrients micronutrients minerals vitamins calories and macronutrients protein fat and carbohydrates of that example of a of eating for me I'm going to use the website chronometer which is free I have no association with chronometer I just find it helpful to track now as I will point out in this video chronometer still has some limitations so be aware of that but I think in general if you put the food you are eating in a day into chronometer you'll get a sense of how many calories you'll get a sense of protein fat carbohydrates and you'll get a sense of where your nutrients might be deficient now I'll go into more detail as we get into this I'll also add that at the end of the video I'm going to compare what I eat in a day to a vegan diet to a well- constructed vegan diet so I found a video from a vegan that looks pretty healthy on YouTube and I put his daily intake of food which is pretty similar to mine in calories actually into chronometer also and I'm going to show you how these two things compare at the end of the video so let's get into what I eat in a day on chronometer and we'll

chunks.title: Breakfast: Raw Goat Milk and Eggs
chunks.summary: For breakfast, the speaker enjoys raw goat milk, honey, and creatine, which they believe supports mental clarity and performance. They follow this with scrambled eggs cooked in tallow.

chunks.title: Lunch: Skirt Steak and Nutrient-Rich Foods
chunks.summary: Lunch consists of skirt steak, raw milk, and half an ounce of beef liver which is described as a nutrient-dense food. The speaker emphasizes the importance of consuming organ meats for their high vitamin content.
chunks.transcript: cows are eating grass it's made from the fat of cows in Australia and the these cows are the happiest cows I've ever seen which is why our Tallow looks the best it's the most orange meaning it has the most beta kerene connected with grass consumption of these cows so I'll cook the eggs in the Tallow I will drink some watermelon juice post surf I might have a mango and I'll cut up some avocado with that for breakfast so that's my breakfast post surf now in terms of lunch I'll eat this in the middle of the day I like skirt steak I like the texture of skirt steak I like that it has more collagen in it I don't want to get purely muscle Cuts I want cuts that are a little hard to chew sometimes that have collagen kind of built into the steak uh sometimes we'll have some parmesano regano cheese with that that cheese is raw from Italy so any parmesano regano cheese you find is raw and I'll have some orange juice which I squeeze fresh I might eat some rambutans which are also called maminos here in Costa Rica and around lunch I might have half an ounce of beef liver with some peeled cucumber so this is all you need for liver is about half an ounce per day that's like a multivitamin and if you don't want to eat like regular fresh liver you can do something like heart and soil supplements very proud of that brand in terms of the quality of the desicated organs that we make in the capsules so whichever way is best for you the fresh liver is better in my opinion but if you can't get that a capsule is not a bad option for you and then I'll go to dinner and I have some ground beef I look for the fattiest ground beef 8020 ground beef I have more goat milk with a little more honey I might have some dragon fruit and watermelon juice for dinner so that's a sample of what I might be eating and you

chunks.title: Dinner: Ground Beef and Hydration
chunks.summary: Dinner typically includes fatty ground beef, more goat milk, and fruits like dragon fruit and watermelon juice. The speaker describes their diet’s caloric intake and macronutrient breakdown.
chunks.transcript: can see here here let's actually get into the nuts and bolts of what that all breaks down to in a day I will often eat around 3,300 calories now for the record I'm 5'9 and I'm about 165 lbs I run about 10% body fat I've done a previous video about a dexa scan so I know that if you guys have seen me with my shirt off I always have abs that's just something that's important to me but I don't really have to think about that very hard because I'm not eating any junk food I eat zero junk food in my life I'm just very disciplined about this and that makes it pretty easy for me to stay lean so 3,300 calories is a lot of calories and I don't gain weight now I also surf 1 and 1/2 2 hours a day and I'm pretty active so make of that what you will I have about 170 grams of protein per day I think in terms of macro breakdown in terms of protein you want to be getting about 8 to one gram of protein per pound of gold body weight I'm about 165 lbs that's right about where I want to be I probably don't even need that much protein the literature suggests that 08 is just fine for me but I end up with around 170 gram of protein per day most days and note that this protein is from animal sources which is much more bioavailable than plant protein that will be relevant later when we look at a sample vegan diet from uh a vegan YouTuber that I found and I have about 255 grams of carbohydrates I am active and I feel very good with this you don't have to eat that many carbohydrates I would say scale your

chunks.title: Caloric and Macronutrient Breakdown
chunks.summary: The speaker shares their average daily caloric intake and macronutrient breakdown, noting their active lifestyle supports a high carbohydrate intake from fruits and honey. They discuss the significance of balancing protein, carbs, and fats as per individual activity levels.
chunks.transcript: carbohydrates based on your activity level but as I've said many times in the past don't fear carbohydrates carbohydrates in the form of fruit and honey get vilified all the time chunks.title: Micronutrients and Vitamins in the Diet
chunks.summary: The discussion shifts to micronutrients, emphasizing the abundance of vitamins from animal sources, especially from organ meats, and the potential deficiencies in vegan diets for certain nutrients like B12 and vitamin A.

chunks.title: Comparative Analysis with Vegan Diet
chunks.summary: The speaker compares their diet to a well-constructed vegan diet, highlighting differences in protein quality and nutrient availability. They express concerns over deficiencies in plant-based diets, especially regarding protein bioavailability and certain vitamins.
chunks.transcript: for conversation about vegan diet in a moment and I would say that they're all of these amino acids are totally well represented in my diet they're all in 3 to 400% I have lots of all the amino acids that I need which are crucial for neurotransmitter synthesis for muscle protein synthesis for making enzymes for being immunologically strong in your body and I have a lot of amino acids that are not even listed here things like carnitine things like ancine things like torine which are consistently associated with longevity again these don't really occur in plant Foods this is one of the places where chronometer starts to fall short in terms of analysis of amino acids they're really just telling you about essential amino acids and non-essential amino acids but they're not telling you about some of the more Nuance amino acids like torine for instance which doesn't you don't get any of on a vegan diet and is very important for humans let's go to vitamins you can see here that I'm getting lots of B vitamins I'm getting over 100% of thiamine I'm getting 273 of ribo flaven nasin pantothenic acid Pur doxine and in contrast to a vegan diet I'm getting a ton of cobalamin which is B12 lots of folate and I'm getting bioavailable vitamin A so I want to highlight this vitamin A is mostly from liver you can see that the majority of the vitamin A I'm getting is from beef chunks.title: Conclusion: Benefits of Animal-Based Diet
chunks.summary: In conclusion, the speaker advocates for an animal-based diet as beneficial for nutrient density and overall health, especially for those struggling with specific dietary issues. They encourage individuals to focus on nutrient-rich food sources while considering their health needs.
chunks.transcript: still not something I'm a fan of and I worry that long term this could make his cell membranes more susceptible to damage because he stuffing them full of omega-6 fatty acids in the USDA guidelines which are much lower than I think it would be ideal so you can see that in terms of protein there's a real problem here this is 50 grams of protein that is not very available I think that adding more protein if you wanted to use plant protein fine but I think you're going to have a lot more problems digesting that it's potentially much more contaminated vegan protein powders we a well constructed vegan diet again I hope Phil is thriving and if he sees this video I'd love to buy him a steak cuz I think he'd be a lot better with that so hopefully that's helpful guys in terms
